As the debate rages on the ground, China has sent its first female astronaut into space.

2012-06-26.title: Taliban Kill 13 Soldiers in Pakistan Raid
2012-06-26.abstract: The Afghan-based militants attacked in a rare cross-border raid. Pakistani officials called in a senior Afghan diplomat to protest the killings.

2012-06-26.title: In Reversal, Ex-President of Paraguay Seeks Power
2012-06-26.abstract: The ousted leader, Fernando Lugo, said he would use resistance to the new establishment to regain power.

2012-06-26.title: Fears Accompany Fishermen in Japanese Disaster Region
2012-06-26.abstract: For the first time since last year’s nuclear catastrophe, commercial fishing has resumed in the waters off Fukushima, raising concerns about radiation contaminating sea produce.

2012-06-26.title: Gunmen Kill 3 Officers at Airport in Mexico
2012-06-26.abstract: The federal police officers were at the busy Mexico City airport intending to arrest people in a drug-trafficking investigation.

2012-06-27.title: Learning About Life on Mars, via a Detour to Mexico
2012-06-27.abstract: The desert near Cuatro Ciénegas, Mexico, is inhabited by organisms able to survive extreme conditions, and scientists say the environment resembles that of Mars billions of years ago.

2012-06-27.title: The World Koran-Reciting Competition in Tehran
2012-06-27.abstract: What one participant called the “Olympic Games of Koran recital,” the Iran International Koran competition, took place in Tehran last week.

2012-06-27.title: Before a Full House in Iran, Koran Reciters Vie for Top Honors
2012-06-27.abstract: National champions from 65 countries, including a 15-year-old from Uganda, traveled to Tehran for the competition.

2012-06-27.title: German Ruling Against Circumcising Boys Draws Criticism
2012-06-27.abstract: In a nation that is home to millions of Muslims and more than 100,000 Jews, the Cologne court’s decision was seen by many as an attack on religious freedom.

2012-06-27.title: Cyprus to Take Over European Presidency
2012-06-27.abstract: What was supposed to be a proud moment for the tiny island nation of Cyprus may be overshadowed by its request for a bailout and its long-simmering feud with Turkey.

2012-06-28.title: A Note to Readers
2012-06-28.abstract: The New York Times introduces a beta version of a new online Chinese-language edition.

2012-06-28.title: On the Farms of France, the Death of a Pixelated Workhorse
2012-06-28.abstract: The Minitel network is to be permanently shut down after three decades of service that have left deep marks on business and culture in France.

2012-06-28.title: Deadly Day for Afghan Police
2012-06-28.abstract: Ten police officers were killed in ambushes and explosions in Afghanistan, mostly in the country’s south and west.

2012-06-28.title: At Outset of Brussels Summit Meeting, the One Thing Missing Is Confidence
2012-06-28.abstract: As European Union leaders prepared for the summit conference in Brussels, there was considerable pessimism but some determination about the possibility of a significant economic breakthrough.

2012-06-28.title: Syrian Arrests Are Said to Have Snared Tens of Thousands
2012-06-28.abstract: An unrelenting campaign of arrests by the Syrian government since the start of the uprising has focused on two groups: men and boys from besieged towns, and secular activists.

2012-06-29.title: A Growing Black Market for Body Parts
2012-06-29.abstract: With Europe roiled by financial upheaval, experts say the black market for selling human organs is expanding as vulnerable, desperately poor people seek to sell their body parts.

2012-06-29.title: Black Market for Body Parts Spreads Among the Poor in Europe
2012-06-29.abstract: A phenomenon’s momentum is abetted by the Internet, a global shortage of transplant organs and, in some cases, unscrupulous traffickers ready to exploit economic misery.

2012-06-29.title: U.S. Exempts Singapore and China on Iran Oil
2012-06-29.abstract: Last-minute waivers will spare China and Singapore from potentially onerous financial penalties required under a strict American law on Iran sanctions.

2012-06-29.title: Database of Veterans’ Medals Cited as Alternative to ‘Stolen Valor’
2012-06-29.abstract: Justice Anthony Kennedy said a “database of medal winners” could offer an alternative solution to the Stolen Valor Act, perhaps a reference to the work of Doug Sterner, a Vietnam veteran who for more than a decade has been painstakingly logging military award citations into a public database.

2012-06-29.title: British Memorial Honors World War II Bomber Crews
2012-06-29.abstract: Queen Elizabeth II inaugurated a monument to the aircrews, who had long lacked such recognition because of unease over civilian deaths from their bombing campaigns.

2012-06-30.title: Mexico Seems Poised to Embrace Party It Ousted in 2000
2012-06-30.abstract: Enrique Peña Nieto, the nominee of the PRI for the Mexican presidency, leads in polls. A victory by him would be a startling reversal for a country that rejected the party in 2000.

2012-06-30.title: U.S. Reaches Out to China, but Not for Naval Maneuvers
2012-06-30.abstract: Chinese analysts said that cooperation was not the main American goal, as a 22-nation war exercise began off Hawaii.

2012-06-30.title: Italy’s Prime Minister Nudges German Chancellor Toward Growth
2012-06-30.abstract: Mario Monti emerged at the European Union summit meeting as the uncontested leader of the “pro-growth” forces, and helped put Italy back on the diplomatic map.

2012-06-30.title: Iranian Says Nuclear Talks Have Reached ‘Critical Point’
2012-06-30.abstract: Iran’s ambassador to the United Nations has denounced sanctions against his country, saying that negotiations over its disputed nuclear program have reached a “critical point.”

2012-06-30.title: On the Road, Cheers for a Kenyan and His Cause
2012-06-30.abstract: Zackary Kimotho is trying to raise $3 million to build a spinal cord rehabilitation center in Kenya by traveling in a wheelchair from Nairobi to Cape Town.
